problem_solved: German companies face complex legal requirements for occupational health and safety, but managing these requirements across multiple locations with qualified specialists is challenging and resource-intensive.
who_buys_it: HR directors, EHS managers, CFOs at German SMEs and large enterprises with 100+ employees across manufacturing, logistics, healthcare, and office-based industries
key_features: Network of 150+ occupational health centers across Germany
Workplace safety assessments and risk analysis
business_model: B2B service contracts - per-examination fees and annual retainer packages for ongoing occupational health support; pricing varies by company size and service scope
why_varden_should_care: Largest occupational medicine provider in Germany with 150+ locations; strong regulatory moat as German occupational health requirements become stricter; serving 40,000+ companies; transitioning from B·A·D to BG prevent shows modernization push; addresses pain point of decentralized occupational health service delivery

problem_solved: Swiss employees and companies need rapid access to medical consultations but face long wait times, travel requirements, and difficulty coordinating care across the healthcare system.
who_buys_it: Swiss health insurance companies (partnering for telemedicine services), HR departments for corporate health programs, and individual patients seeking convenient care access
business_model: B2B2C - partnerships with Swiss health insurers who offer Medgate as part of insurance plans; per-consultation fees covered by mandatory Swiss health insurance; corporate wellness contracts with employers
why_varden_should_care: Market leader in Swiss telemedicine with 25+ years experience; 95% patient satisfaction rate; strong B2B partnerships with major Swiss insurers; handles 60,000+ consultations during peak periods; expanding Kids Line shows focus on family health; addresses Swiss market where telemedicine adoption is government-supported

why_varden_should_care: One of Germany's leading telemedicine platforms; addresses German market where 70+ million people have statutory health insurance; regulatory-compliant telemedicine approach; helps employers reduce productivity loss from medical appointments; strong growth potential as Germany embraces digital health

why_varden_should_care: 94% recommendation rate from 150,000+ patients served; 1 in 3 diagnoses corrected by specialists shows impact on quality; data-driven approach to specialist selection; helps self-insured employers reduce costs through better care navigation; addresses German market complexity where finding specialists is challenging
